reviews.title: Unfortunate popping noises while listening to music
reviews.text: Initially I LOVED this speaker.  It was inexpensive and the sound was impressive for its size.  Pairing with my iPhone 7 was no problem and the battery lasts a long time.  My issue with this is that after a few weeks I started hearing a popping noise every now and then.  I checked to see if it was maybe my MP3 player but heard no popping sounds when I listened through headphones.  I hoped my speaker was just defective and I exchanged it but the new one still has the same popping noise.  It happens when using an MP3 connected via aux and while using Bluetooth as well.  So disappointed.

reviews.title: A great little  - BIG speaker
reviews.text:
I was really amazed at the sound! A tiny speaker the size of a soda can that creates a wonderful sound environment. I have it hooked to my Echo dot and the sound clarity is really good! And the bass coming out of the top is pretty precise. It's not the thundering bass but the correct one (to my ears). Takes up really a very small space.

Set up was very easy. Pressed a button to pair it with Echo using Bluetooth. My phone and Echo both picked it up really quick. Can't really tell if it's going to work in long distance but I have no reason to doubt what the manufacturer is claiming.

The only issue I had was figuring out how to use the volume buttons. I assume it's a quick press to lower or increase the volume but it's actually a long press. And then I am not sure how long to hold down the button to increase and decrease since there's no visual cue. But once I found the right volume it's OK. I can control the actual volume from the source as well.

I got the black one so it sits very nicely behind a few Knick Knacks and not really visible. The sound is "surround" so it's not directional. I have not paired it up with a twin to test out the stereo effect. But that's not how I intend to use this speaker anyways.

Very happy with this purchase. Kudos to the engineers for putting together such a nice speaker!

reviews.title: I didn’t know how much I would love this speaker.
reviews.text:
I have had one of these speakers for a year now and I use it nearly every day! It is perfect for listening to music in the shower; no more straining to hear the phone on max volume!

I brought it with me to a holiday lunch meeting at work in one of our event spaces and one of my bosses was so surprised that it wasn’t the speakers in the space that he bought one. Other co workers that weren’t there reached out to me to tell me they bought one because of my boss’s speaker and story about mine. Very odd, but it seems that people decide that they may actually need a speaker after seeing this one.

This speaker, while being hefty in the hand (I’d say it weighs about a pound), is super packable. It’s durability leaves me unconcerned when I toss it in my carryon bag for work trips or visits to far away family. It is certainly a luxury, but also fun to pull out and toss on the patio at a family gathering.

I just picked up a second speaker (yay for holiday sales). I like party mode and the fact that you could use 2 for stereo audio if you are so inclined. I did note that the audio was oddly out of sync when I first tried it, but repairing seemed to resolve it.

I recommend to anyone in the market for a Bluetooth speaker, but admit it is more of a luxury item.

reviews.title: Will pair with only one device at a time; sounds horrible
reviews.text:
In full disclosure, I must admit that I miss my Bose Bluetooth speaker. Their acoustics are impeccable. The "Tribit XSound Go Bluetooth Speakers" only cost a fraction of my Bose speaker. I first purchased the "DOSS Touch Wireless Bluetooth" which is in the same price range; I returned that product because it lacked bass and was unimpressive overall. The Tribit, however, has plenty of bass for such a small speaker. Ironically, the problem that I have is the treble! I think it is an inherent problem with smaller speakers and not this particular device. I have installed an equalizer on my computer which mitigates some of the problems and makes the speaker sound very acceptable.

The pros:
• The speaker is loud! It is perfect for a jam session in your bedroom. I'm not sure it is suitable for large or multi rooms.
• The quality is good, and the speaker looks like a million bucks.
• It has an excellent microphone that works well with phone calls and even to interact with Siri or Cortana.
• The Play button allows one to answer, reject or end a call. It also can play or pause a song. Finally, it allows you to skip a music track forward or backward.

The cons:
• While the bass is fairly impressive, the treble is slightly overriding.
• The battery does not nearly last for 24 hours if you like to blast music as I do
• The speaker can only be paired to one device at a time. If you are listening to music on the computer and the phone tries to connect, the speaker will sometimes disconnect from all devices. The speaker does have an av input one could connect to the computer but obviously, the speaker would no longer be completely portable. This could be a deal breaker for many.

After comparing this speaker with others in its price range, I realize that this speaker has such poor sound quality that I could never recommend it to anyone.

reviews.title: Muddy Sound Qualuty for JBL
reviews.text: JBL's speaker products are usually quite good if you are willing to pay the price.  This speaker however doesn't sound so great even considering size.  Usually small speakers tent to be too bright.  This is the opposite.  Though not a boomer, it is pretty strong in the bass for it's size.  But using an eq to emphasize the mid-upper range only helps a little.  You cannot get a balanced spectrum.  If this were a $30 speaker that would be no big deal, but for #70 and the JBL name, it is a let down.  It can go quite loud for it's size though.

reviews.title: Some flaws, great sound.
reviews.text:
Sound is good. Quite good. Very good. I am not an audiophile, but imho it's the best sound I have ever heard. Rich, juicy, loud, deep. No distortion that I can feel. Some files sound worse than others, but listen. The compression quality of the audio file plays a role, too.
Design is good. Looks modern, aesthetic, solid sturdy construction.
Now, it would never hold up for 20 hours. Under no circumstances. At most 5-6 hours in average, on medium volume.
Also, it did not connect correctly to my Alexa dot. After connection the dot and the speaker both remained silent. I was counting to use it with Alexa.
Finally, absence of AUX socket is a disadvantage. I would love to use the speaker with my old Zishan, but there is no way.
So, I will keep it to use with my cell for parties, but I am not completely happy with the features it offers.

reviews.title: Good speaker.
reviews.text: I had higher expectations for this speaker. Bluetooth works well, looks nice and seems pretty sturdy. The sound is really pretty good from low to mid-volume. Beyond that it just gets louder, not better. From what I've read the downloadable app can help modify the sound but is not without it's own drawbacks but I have not tried it myself. I want to plug and play, not fiddle. Kept it plugged in the first few hours of use. Next use the battery ran for more than 8 hours at better than half volume with no signs of wearing down. Placement of this speaker is an issue for me. In close proximity to the action you'll need to turn it down or conversation is challenging. The sound is very "directional". You can close your eyes and pinpoint precisely where this thing is situated from any position. I did however place it near the rear of my yard at about two thirds volume and it kept us entertained on our deck all day. I'm thinking the addition of two more of these or the smaller JBL's would create very satisfactory listening experience throughout the home but then price becomes an issue. At $300 this is not cheap but I'd say depending on your expectations it's worth the price.
reviews.author: AGPARXP2XP4ULCKYZHBODESUVNCQ

reviews.title: Loud, long battery
reviews.text: Here we go! I bought this because my brother has one. I knew what i was getting into first hand before purchase. These speakers are great. Despite the negative feedbacks on audio. Listen, unless you're a musical prodigy who ONLY has the BEST of the BEST audio equipment and likes to spend HELLA money on top line overdone products, then this speaker will blow you away. The sound is crisp, the speaker is durable, my brother and his friend submerged their speakers under water for a while and it was tip top. This speaker is well worth it. A little heavy and bulky for portable, but i dont see any reason to strap this onto a bicycle (however it can be done).  The only negatives i can say, is the speaker I received had a few manufactuer flaws. The emblem is kinda not even on the front and the flap that covers the USB/charging port doesnt stay closed. Thinking this one will not be waterproof because of that. On my brothers xtreme 2 , the flap that covers the USB/Charging port is HARD to open, mine is almost too easy, almost feels like its assisted lol.  Anyways, despite that, this speaker is awesome!

Cons

reviews.title: SO FAR SO GOOD
reviews.text: I haven't tried to see the water resistance factor.  The sound quality is amazing.  I like that there are + -' volume controls on it.  So I can increase or decrease the volume from my "device" AND the JBL.  I also like that it has an automatic turn off feature, if I forget to turn it off.  The battery charger is good.  Although it doesn't come with a wall or electric outlet adapter.  I had a couple extra laying around.  So no problem.  You may want to consider a longer battery charger USB cable.  Which will give you more flexibility on where you listen, when on battery.  It does carry a five hour charge.  A few days ago I was on a phone call on 'Bluetooth' with it and took it outside to go to the mail box, while on the phone.  I left my cell in my house and walked about 100 feet outside to my mailbox and the phone quality stayed the same.  I highly recommend this item.  My girlfriend has one she paid $200 for and the sound isn't as good as this $30 product.

title: Bose SoundLink Color Bluetooth Speaker (Blue)
description: If music is your motivation, wait until you hear the Bose Sound Link Color Bluetooth speaker. It plays your favorite tunes from your favorite Bluetooth device—out loud in clear, full audio—just about anywhere you want to go. It's small, durable and simple to use. And it comes in a spectrum of colors to match almost any style. The Sound Link Color speaker suits your active, mobile lifestyle. The body is constructed of durable materials, and its rounded, compact shape fits easily into your bag or backpack. The Sound Link Color speaker has a range of about 30 feet, so you can enjoy your music out loud, without wires. It pairs with your smartphone, tablet or other Bluetooth device. Voice prompts talk you through Bluetooth pairing, making it simple. And the speaker remembers the eight most recent devices you've used, so reconnecting is even easier. The speaker can connect to two Bluetooth devices at the same time, letting you switch between them quickly—when the first device stops playing, the second can start. The Sound Link Color speaker lets you play your music wherever you are, whatever you're doing. It's compact and light enough (1.25 pounds) to take along even when you're traveling light. The speaker is designed for ease of use. It features power, volume and source buttons. You control other functions from your Bluetooth device. Don't let the Sound Link Color speaker's small size fool you. It may be compact, but it delivers a satisfying listening experience. Proprietary technologies and a unique design produce clear, well-balanced, full-range sound. More features: Auxiliary input enables you to connect to other audio sources, like a tablet or MP3 player, Charges with most USB power sources or the included wall charger and Micro USB port allows for charging and software updates. What's in the box: Sound Link Color Bluetooth speaker, Wall charger and Cable. Clear, full-range sound you might not expect from a compact speaker Voice prompts make pairing your devices eas

reviews.text:
This BOSE SOUNDLINK COLOR BLUETOOTH SPEAKER may not be the ideal portable speaker for serious audiophiles--because it has no treble/base buttons, only volume buttons--but I love mine. The sound is rich and full, whether it's paired with my old Sony Bravia TV, iPad2, or Kindle Fire, or connected by AV stereo cable (3.5mm M/M) to my MP3 player's headphone jack. The volume is controlled by the device the speaker is connected to, or by the plus/minus volume buttons on the speaker itself. The speaker also has an Aux button for the direct cable connection, and a Skip button that lets you go forward or backward in your music selections.

This Bose portable speaker can support two Bluetooth-connected devices at once, so you can switch between two devices (e.g., an iPad and a Kindle Fire) without repeating the pairing process every time. A great feature! The speaker automatically connects with the last-connected Bluetooth device, and switches devices when you press the Bluetooth button. The speaker's voice prompts help you with pairing, and tell you which two devices are currently paired. The voice prompts can be disabled, if you wish.

This Bose speaker is definitely an improvement over the speakers built into my old Sony Bravia TV. Voices are much clearer--including the dialogue from British TV shows--and the sound is far richer. Of course, using this speaker instead of a TV sound bar is only a temporary solution, because the speaker needs regular recharging. When fully charged, it delivers sound for many hours (up to 8) and outlasts the charge on the [[ASIN:B004B8GF7Y TaoTronics® Bluetooth Transmitter]] that I added to my non-Bluetooth-enabled TV.

Whether the TaoTronics transmitter was connected to the TV's headphone jack or to the TV's AV ouputs, this speaker paired instantly. However, when the TV transmitter was connected to the AV outputs, the speaker produced some static unless it was positioned close to the TV. When the TV transmitter was connected to the headphone jack, the speaker produced no static at all, no matter where it was positioned (within the Bluetooth range).

This Bose speaker charges quickly with the included AC adapter and USB cable. Pressing the speaker's power button produces a battery indicator that glows red, amber, or green to indicate the current level of charge. The speaker is lightweight enough to carry around in a backpack or tote bag. There is a case available (see [[ASIN:B00NJGRKG4 Bose SoundLink Color carry case]]), but I don't think a case is needed, because the speaker seems to be sturdily constructed.
reviews.author: AEYGPUCRKH7G4VM22FM3VAKSQ23Q

reviews.title: All good in a small package
reviews.text:
I listen mostly to jazz in the background while I’m reading or on the web, usually at low volume - sometimes rock at louder volumes. I’ve bought any number of blue tooth speakers, as a hobby I suppose, always in search of that nirvana sound. They’ve ranged from really small Fugoo units (with unbelievably good sound) to paired floor standing auditorium behemoths that will blow your contacts out. IMO, the UE Boom 3’s are not only a good value, they are superlative in sound from low to high ranges at easy and medium volume. Great for voice, too, e.g. podcasts. I have 2 paired and will likely buy another pair (stereo is nice) for another room.

I recently tried the UE Megablast and they don’t hold a candle to to crispness and range of the Boom 3’s. They’re loud and have some nice control features but for sound, IMO the 3’s are the best by far, and uncannily the least expensive of over 10 other brands, up to $300. Unless you’re filling a large room with loud music I’d say the Boom is the best speaker in it’s portable class. Enjoy, and don’t worry about missing any sound - you’ll pleasantly hear it all!
reviews.author: AEKGEHD6XSWX34ICGNG3YY3TBUKA

reviews.title: Beware this device cannot do deep base
reviews.text:
I know it's a little speaker and I shouldn't expect booming bass from it, but that's not the problem. The problem is that any bass tones below 90 Hz aren't low power, they don't exist ... at all. Everything below 90Hz is just gone, like a high-pass filter. Classical music sounds retarded when you can't even hear the cello or kettle drums. Rap sounds even more retarded, like the entire percussion track has been removed from the recording.

I don't know how people enjoy listening to music on these things. I can only guess that it's the same type of people who can't tell the difference between a low-quality 128 kbps MP3 and a lossless FLAC file.

reviews.text:
I have tested and reviewed hundreds of Bluetooth speakers and while the vast majority are quite average - this one has really impressed me.

First of all it is IPX7 waterproof (safe to be immersed up to 1m for 30 minutes underwater) which is among the highest ratings I’ve found for this type of speaker.

It employs Bluetooth 5.0 technology which will allow for excellent transmission from a solid 30 feet away. And this speaker will even allow pairing with another for true stereo sound!

I have fully tested the microphone and it is adequate for making calls and controlling Siri but I would not say it is exceptional.

The “party lights” I personally find gimmicky and a waste of battery resources.

The battery life is exceptional, lasting a solid 8 hours before needing to be recharged.

This is an all around excellent speaker that packs a lot of sound into a relatively small package and has excellent battery life.

Whether you need a Bluetooth speaker or you are looking for a gift this is an ideal choice.

Minuses

reviews.title: USPS LLV Comparison: OontZ Angle 3 Plus vs Anker SoundCore
reviews.text:
First off, I am not an audiophile. This is a review from a simple person who wanted music at work.

I am a Rural Carrier for the United Stated Postal Service. I deliver mail in an LLV truck and these vehicles are basically mobile tin cans. Every panel rattles, making for quite an unpleasant environment, audio wise. I tried playing music directly off my phone, but alas my speakerphone is only so loud and is constantly drowned out by the LLV.

So, I researched Bluetooth speakers and, based on the reviews and popularity, recently purchased two:
-[[ASIN:B016XTADG2 Anker SoundCore Bluetooth Speaker with 24-Hour Playtime, 66-Foot Bluetooth Range & Built-in Mic, Dual-Driver Portable Wireless Speaker with Low Harmonic Distortion and Superior Sound - Black]]
-[[ASIN:B01LZV6TYQ OontZ Angle 3 PLUS Splashproof Bluetooth Speaker - Designed & Engineered by Cambridge SoundWorks for Richer Bass, HD Sound, and 30-Hour Playtime, 10W+ Power, Water Resistant, Portable Wireless Speaker]]

The Anker SoundCore is being returned while I am keeping the OontZ Angle 3 Plus and here is why.

I tested both speakers in my home at first. My very first impression, out of the box:
OontZ Angle 3 Plus - was nice to touch and just felt solid and very well built.
Anker SoundCore - just felt like a simple lightweight brick. It really didn't give the impression of longevity or durability.

The set-up guides were quite different from each other.
OontZ Angle 3 Plus - Extremely easy to read, follow and understand (remember, I am not a music nut lol. This was my first time ever using a Bluetooth speaker and was in completely uncharted territory.) It was all in English, and only English and the diagrams were detailed and made the entire set-up process seamless and carefree.
Anker SoundCore - This guide was very hard to follow. It is printed in darn near every language, in tiny print and the diagrams were quite small as well. I ultimately did get the speaker paired with my phone and figured out what buttons did what, but it took nearly 15 minutes to do so.

Bluetooth Pairing
With both speakers and my phone lined up on a table (well within the proximity specified in each set-up guide), both speakers paired, however:
OontZ Angle 3 Plus - MUCH faster in pairing (mere seconds!).
Anker SoundCore - took nearly two minutes to complete the pairing process.

Pause/Play Button
Since I dismount to deliver packages to a customer's door routinely on my route, I really need to have a simply way to pause my music. I really like how both speakers offer this function on directly on the units, saving me from having to handle my phone.
OontZ Angle 3 Plus - I barely press the Play/Pause button and the speaker immediately reacts. There is NO hesitation.
Anker SoundCore - I press the Play/Pause button and there is a delay of about 5 seconds.. just long enough to make me think I didn't press hard enough and reach to do it again only for the speaker to finally react.

Volume/Overall Playback Quality
OontZ Angle 3 Plus - Wow. Just wow. It is truly unbelievable to hear such full sound, and bass(!), coming from such a small unit. I found myself perfectly pleased with the output without ever reaching max volume on the speaker itself. So I am looking forward to how well it will work in my LLV during my first road test. As for now, I am thoroughly impressed!
Anker SoundCore - I maxed out the volume on my phone and the speaker and was just not impressed. If I could guess, I'd say the max output this speaker achieved was only about 25% more than my speakerphone alone (while the OontZ Angle 3 Plus blew it out of the water with close to double, if not more, output than my speakerphone alone).
I also noted some kind of distortion or interference type background sound with the Anker SoundCore at mid volume and above.

Speakerphone/Mic
I was able to answer an incoming phone call on both units as described in each set-up guide. The quality of in-call sound on each speaker differed:
OontZ Angle 3 Plus - My husband's (the caller) voice was crisp and clear, as if he was sitting talking right next to me. There was no distortion or feedback out of the speaker. Husband reported nothing but a clear voice on his phone.
Anker SoundCore - Husband's voice was clear, but echoed horribly. No matter what volume level I used. There was no distortion (I was expecting this given the distortion with the music playback) bu the continuous echoing is a major issue for me. Husband reported a slight "faded" type of echo on his phone.

Road Test (in my LLV)
OontZ Angle 3 Plus - The sound is amazing in the truck! Thinking I would need it, I maxed the volume on both the speaker and my phone then pressed play on my music. Well, I certainly don't need max volume. I reduced the volume on both the speaker and my phone and found a very happy medium output. This speaker performs perfectly in the loud, rattling setting of a USPS LLV.. even while bouncing down rural dirt two tracks and washboard gravel roads. I am extremely pleased with this tiny speaker.
Anker Soundcore - Even with maxed volume settings, I could not get this speaker to perform anywhere near as well as the OontZ Angle 3 Plus. It had volume and was loud enough to hear over the vehicle's own noise, however the distortion/interference mentioned earlier was still present and in the truck during the road test it had what I would describe as an echoing effect on the music, specifically the singers' voices. It just wasn't clear. (I hope that makes sense.)

Battery Life/Listen While Charging
OontZ Angle 3 Plus - So far, the battery life is impressive. After receiving it, I charged it overnight (as recommended). I then had it connected to my phone for about an hour of home testing. Turned it off and I next used it at work on a Saturday.. had it powered on and connected to my phone and actively playing music for about 5.5 hours. I then used it again on Monday (much longer and busier days in the USPS, FYI).. had it on, connected and active continuously for nearly 9 hours. I have yet to see the low battery light come on. I plan to see just how many days of work I can get out of a single charge.
Anker Soundcore - I have no information for this section on this speaker since I am returning it.

Exterior/Cleaning
At first, I had the OontZ Angle 3 Plus setting on the dashboard of the LLV. This didn't last more than two stops. Despite having the four rubber "feet", the speaker still slid around causing more of a distraction than I would like. I ended up setting it on top of my personal bag, in a bin by my feet with the speaker facing up. This move really helped my piece of mind, knowing it will not go anywhere. This speaker has a soft rubber exterior. It is quite nice and feels luxurious, however... this exterior attracts all dust particles within 50 feet! USPS LLVs are *not* known for being the cleanest and even though I moved the speaker from the dust-ridden dashboard to a bin, it was still slathered with dust and dirt by the end of my route. I figured it was a fair trade, sacrificing the appearance for the gain of having decent music. But.. I got home and went to clean it. I will tell you, my expectations of this working were very low. I didn't think the dust would come off that supple rubber. But with a wet paper towel and a few slow wipes, it did. So, it can be cleaned up!

Case
I did purchase the case for this speaker ([[ASIN:B01NBDY6VP [Official] OontZ Angle 3 Plus Bluetooth Portable Speaker Carry Case, Neoprene with Aluminum Carabiner, reinforced zipper, by Cambridge SoundWorks [NOT FOR OontZ Angle 3]]]). I was mainly wanting a safe way to transport and store the speaker, but after reading the other reviews I was hoping I would be able to keep it clean in the case and still enjoy my music on my route. I received the case and used it yesterday. The case is extremely nice and I appreciate the interior mesh pocket. I have no need for it now, but I'm sure it will come in handy if I ever take this speaker on vacation and need to take the charging cables along. As for playback while in the case? Yeah, it was terribly muffled. It didn't work for me as it apparently has for others. Yet, the case itself is exactly what I was wanting for taking it to and from work. It is not as thickly padded as I would have expected, however it does protect the speaker.

Overall, for my specific purpose and intended use for a Bluetooth speaker, the Anker SoundCore simply won't cut it. However, it is still a nice speaker and will perhaps fit your needs perfectly. After all, most people don't drive LLVs and have the same high demands that go along with that. For me, the OontZ Angle 3 Plus is what I'll be bopping down the road to, mailbox to mailbox.

Happy hunting! Hope this review helps you out!
reviews.author: AGMZO7NPGHYPLCOCLMQEDGKNCHVA

reviews.title: A good compact stereo bluetooth speaker
reviews.text:
I own the A3143 Premium Stereo Bluetooth speaker (2x10W=20W) which sounds great, but it doesn't have a microphone or the longer battery life of this lower-cost A3102 SoundCore speaker (2x3W=6W). The features and sound characteristics are different, but which is the better choice depends on personal preference. To me based on sound quality alone, the A3143 wins hands down. The bass is stronger (more power is required to drive the lower frequencies than the higher frequencies), but I also find overall frequency response to be more balanced and accurate for instruments and vocals. For either speaker, I'd recommend using an EQ app to set the frequency response and spatial effects to your preference (I use Bass EQ from Coocent). Interestingly, the maximum volume level for both the A3143 and this A3102 SoundCore are about the same.

The A3102 has a brighter sound overall. The A3102 is good as a general purpose speaker, and it is excellent to use with a cell phone. The A3102 microphone (which the A3143 doesn't have) and speaker quality for voice calling are crisp and clear. My dad who can't hear very well often could not understand me on my cell phone handset or its speakerphone, but had no problem when I used the A3102.

So for long battery life, good overall music speaker, and particularly for use as a microphone/speakerphone for voice calls, A3102 is a good choice. For best music reproduction, the A3143. For either, Anker's quality and customer support are among the best in the industry.

reviews.title: Very pretty- instructions unclear. Read on to find out how to play out of 2 speakers simultaneously!
reviews.text:
My mom ordered 2 of these thinking she'd be able to play music out of both of them at the same time from her iPhone. They come in the mail and we cannot figure out how to do this at all, and the instructions that came with them were unclear, as well as what we found online.
After a few hours of trial and error, this is what we figured out:
Pair both of the speakers to the iPhone
Make sure *one* of the speakers in connected (the other may not show anymore/doesn't have to be connected)
On the *connected* speaker, press the play and + buttons simultaneously (a sound will play)
On the other speaker, press the play and + buttons simultaneously (the same sound will play with some additional beeps)

Play music and they should work! This worked best for us when only one iPhone was connected/paired with the speakers at a time.

Hope this helps someone else!

reviews.title: Exceeded my expectations with clean balanced sound and undistorted bass.
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:4c60e87ead4c44abcba14a2d6daa5b1e]] When I test speakers I play the type of music that is meant for headphones because of the meticulous care taken when mixing the recordings. I don't expect headphone quality, but I do expect balanced sound and on occasion, a little extra "undistorted" bass. I do like my bass and the Doss Soundbox Pro+ did not disappoint. The bass was punchy and clean without being overwhelming.
Not only did this fill my living room with clear, undistorted sound, but I could hear it two rooms away without it sounding too bright or boomy at max volume. I am not sure if this would be loud enough to blast through a large room filled with noisy people, but it's perfect for smaller gatherings and apartments that share common walls with neighbors.
I mostly use this with my computer, so the even though the lights are fun, they're a bit distracting near my screen, and I was able to turn it off by holding the "extra bass" button for a few seconds. This speaker paired quickly with all of my devices, and the sound quality exceeded my expectations, I highly recommend it.

reviews.title: Great sound, but don't use the 3.5mm plug!
reviews.text:
I purchased this unit about 4 months ago. I've loved using it streaming from my Mac or iPhone -- great quality sound, easy connection, flawless. Then I made the mistake of plugging it into a device without Bluetooth via the 3.5mm cable. DON'T DO IT!

There is a well-known problem (search the Jawbone support forums) with these Jambox units. The 3.5mm connection takes priority over Bluetooth connections. Unfortunately, the device detects a 3.5mm connection through a set of pins inside the port that can, after a single use (like mine), not fully close again when you've removed the plug. This means the device is hopelessly stuck thinking you have a 3.5mm cable plugged in.

I've contacted Jawbone, but they want me to pay to ship the unit back to them. That's completely unacceptable for a product I spent over a hundred dollars on. This is a known defect and Jawbone should be ashamed that they are still selling devices with that issue. I regret purchasing this instead of a competitor device. The audio quality is great, but the build quality is terrible!
reviews.author: AHLBPBMJCJT3FAQWY23JQEWTU7BQ

This thing has changed my life! Great sound from my iPhone in a very portable package.

Now I can connect my phone to any radio station by wifi and listen on my Jambox. I have replaced the radios in my bathroom, bedroom and kitchen with this phone-Jambox combination.

Highly recommended!

reviews.title: Great "In-The-Middle" Speaker.  Not too small...Not too large.
reviews.text:
1 3.75
 
Bang & Olufsen offer a variety of portable speakers. This is the “middle-of-the-road” speaker. Meaning it’s not the smallest, but not the largest. Their smallest is the Beoplay P2 which will fit in the palm of your hand. Great for a desk, and when you want a speaker so small you can fit it into your pocket. But it doesn’t have the power / sound for larger spaces. The large Beolit 17 is a “party-in-a-box” kind of speaker for full-sound on a patio. .. but. . it’s much larger and not something you can put in a backpack. This Beosound A1 is what “fits in the middle”. It’s small enough to put it into a backpack. It gives a nice amount of power / sound for a small group on a beach. . . dorm room. . . and so on.

I also have the 1st generation A1, so I’m familiar with this speaker. The A2 has some nice upgrades. First of all, it’s now waterproof. Up to 1 meter of water for 30-minutes. So you can use it in the shower. . .by the pool. . .or at the beach without a problem. BUT. . . The speaker comes with a leather strap. It looks great, but if you’re going to be getting this speaker wet a lot, then you might want to change the strap.

Another thing they have improved on are the buttons. On the first edition, you didn’t “feel” anything when you pressed them, so it was hard to know if anything was happening or not. Now you feel the “click”. Having said that. . . . because the buttons are around the edge, you still have to hold the speaker. . . turn it around. . . and find the button you want to press. Honestly, that’s kind of annoying. But you can also use the B&O app to operate the speaker. The app also gives you options for different listening “styles” and such.

You can also set the speaker up to use with Alexa, if you have it.

This speaker works very well as a speakerphone as well. If you’re going to use it as a speakerphone, you’ll want to go into the B&O app and select “Speech” as the “listening mode”.

This speaker is not light. It has a weight of 1-pound 3.75oz. But it’s a much more robust small speaker than other. . . lighter. . . cheaper. . . speakers of the same size. This speaker measures 5.25” across and 2” high.

This speaker comes in two colors. . Anthracite (black) and Grey Mist (silver). They are under different listings.

The base of the speaker is kind of “rubbery”. It’s kind of “anti-slip”, but it is almost smooth, so it will still slide down something at a sharp angle.

Though there is a “flashing light” on the speaker to let you know the battery status. . . The easiest way to check is through the B&O app.

After receiving, and downloading the app, there will probably be a firmware update for the speaker. This took about a half-hour to install.

Made in China.
reviews.author: AG375WAXLZ7PIOQKIQ6KQB4J3JVQ

This immersive, three-dimensional listening experience brings incredible dep

reviews.title: Dats Da Facts, Jack
reviews.text:
The first thing I noticed when I opened the box was how small the Big Jambox is. It's truly portable and the name is more accurate of its sound quality. The first thing I noticed when I turned on the unit was that it talked to me and charted out the steps to pair the speaker to the iPhone 5. The second thing I noticed was the sound. The big full sound. I have truly eclectic tastes. My music collection includes rock, blues, jazz, world music from Africa, the Middle East, and the Lapland of Norway. I listen to didgeridoos from Australia, fiddles, marimbas, and jaw harps from Kyrzykstan. The quality is so good Im a bit blown away by the technology. The clarity is a wonder when listening to vocal qualities of choir ensembles, Bobbie McFerrin, , solo clarinets, and horn sections as well as my favorite twangy African and Middle Eastern instruments. The bass is great considering the speakers size. I predict I will enjoy my symphonic classical music more from now on.
But be reasonable, put it in perspective. Don't expect your Big Jambox to take the place of the power of a live stadium U2 concert or the knock-you -on-your-ass sound wall that passes through your body from a live large brass section of a jazz band orchestra, or be loud enough to overwhelm 10 or 20 loud drunk party goers. Don't be a bonehead! It flabberghasts me that anyone would criticize this speaker. If you actually listen to the sound quality of singers and instruments, or enjoy deep full sounds, spend the money (im on the cheap side) and get the Big Jambox. And dats da facts Jack.
reviews.author: AFAPQNZVNH5UMLMHS3AQP7RAVUXQ

reviews.title: Beware!
reviews.text:
Jawbone products start out great but eventually you will find that you are unable to pair them with your devices. Whether you have the Mini or Big, you will have the same problem. Jawbone will tell you that the problem lies within your Mac and that you must purchase an adaptor (from them). I have three other bluetooth devices that never fail to pair with my Macbook Pro.
Bullpoop! It is a flaw in their product. Trust me as I have wasted close to $400 with their products. Check online and you will find that this is a common problem. Great at first, then, total failure.
I'm about to replace them both since they are now useless.
reviews.author: AE3COLCGH52RGV722SRFZ44PFVRQ

reviews.text:

The TDK A33 sounds WAY better than this speaker, it's water proof and it's less than half the price, but its battery sucks... :(

I need a better product.

reviews.title: Crisper than Bose (so better for podcasts and movies), louder than Sony, and all around great sound
reviews.text:
I love it. Loud and clear, great for music, but also podcasts and movies. Crisper sound quality than Bose (brassy/bassy, so was not as good for podcasts/movies) and more powerful than Sony (which maybe had the best overall sound quality but was not nearly as powerful, and was not quite small enough to grab it and trek around to different rooms, which I do all the time w/ the Big Jambox). Tried all 3 for a week and this won.

My only slight complaint - there is that static noise at very low levels, so if you're using it to listen to music quietly next to your bed, might want to consider another option. But I listen to podcasts with it 5 ft away and it's great.

reviews.title: The sound is nice but for the price it is expected
reviews.text:
It takes sd cards, 3.5mm jack aux input, Bluetooth and nfc pairing. I have used it via Bluetooth and SD card and the sound is very nice.

I recommend using a high amp charging block or charger like the ones for the Samsung tablets or the big apple chargers. I have also seen some laptops with USB-c that would work which would definitely increase the speed of charging. The first time I charged it the battery light was at about 2/3 full and it took almost 6 hours to fully charge it with a small charger.

I like the color green so this speaker is very nice to me.
You could also charge something with this speaker like a phone or small device. I have not tried it but it seems like it would work obviously it would reduce your play time.

Also, I did not know this when I bought it but if you have two of these speakers you can pair them to have true stereo sound.

reviews.title: Better sound than the $300 Bose, but heavier and less portable, and less battery life.
reviews.text:
Better sound than the $300 Bose, but heavier and less portable, and less battery life.

I compared the two side-by-side at a Best Buy store. And it was an easy call the onyx had better sound.. And the onyx seemed powerful enough to overcome the ambiance of the huge store ( perhaps the Bose would fare better against the onyx in a home setting).

The Bose is much more portable, and has significantly better battery life. If I had to carry this once a week to a party I would choose the Onyx. The only way I would choose the Bose, is if it I had to carry it around more than once a week. Or if four hours of batteries was not enough. Also being smaller the Bose would fit better in luggage if you travel a lot.

reviews.title: An excellent product - great speaker - Five stars!
reviews.text:
The UE Roll 2 arrived and in about 3 minutes I was listening to crystal clear music. Now, it takes a while to fully charge so I had to plug it into my computer to help get it charged (it will take probably 5 hours to fully charge the speaker), but right now as I type this review? I have enjoyed listening all afternoon now.

The speaker comes with no instructions. You might want to use a YouTube video to learn a bit more about the device. The "plus" and "minus" on the speaker are the volume up/down buttons and the power button is on the back.

This is a very lightweight speaker that fits in your hand. It is water resistant (it also comes with a small inflatable floatie to place it in while you are in the pool). Still, I'm not fond of the idea of dropping it into a tub of water and waiting to see if it still works after a few minutes of being soaked. LOL.

Seriously, it is easy to use with great sound and appears to be very very well made. I took a couple of pictures of this UE Roll 2 as it is currently plugged into my computer (to charge it) while it plays music from my iphone. The box indicates music can be played from up to 100 feet away from the device. Right now, my iphone is across the room from where I am so I cannot attest to the 100 feet assertion on the box right now.

Would I buy another of these? Absolutely. Just the fact it is compact, good price and apparently great quality makes this one of those MUST have blue tooth speakers for your home, near the pool and it would be easy to pack and take on trips.

Five big stars for this top notch device.

reviews.title: Great sounding speaker & the suction cup works great
reviews.text: I’ve only used this with Apple products, but it’s been super easy to set up and use. It charges by cable, so no batteries required. The suction cup works well. I stuck it to my window over my bed to listen to music from my phone and it sounded great. It wasn’t hollow sounding, and the bass was decent enough that I didn’t have that weird echo when the treble is too high. In the shower it did take some work to get it to stick to the wall (it’s an old fiberglass shower), but it did eventually take. It’s nice to be able to be on a call on the speaker, or listen to music and actually hear it over the water. It’s completely waterproof as far as I have experienced. I enjoy using this speaker and would recommend it for quality and ease of use.

reviews.title: I’m impressed!
reviews.text: [[VIDEOID:7789e37a749fe4c1b759ab761f374d03]] I had never heard of this brand before and was skeptical about this speaker. I own several Bose and we have Sonos and such, so I didn’t have high hopes for this brand ive never heard of. As soon as it arrived I charged it for just over five hours to reach full charge. I immediately took it outside to test it out. I paired my phone with Bluetooth which is obviously simple. I was the able to use Alexa and my unlimited amazon music subscription to play whatever I wanted to hear. I tested music from several genres. I was highly impressed with this speaker. I was shocked with how good it actually sounds! I listened to my video, doesn’t even begin to do it justice. My husband tossed it into the pool and took a video. I’ll have him send it to me so I can add it to my review. It still played in the pool and played well when we took it out. We listened to it all night and even had a phone charging on it. Never lost battery life yet. Probably ran it for 7 hours straight and charged a phone and had the lights on and it never died. It’s extremely well constructed. The handle provides a great grip. So even if your hands are wet, you won’t drop it. And even if you do, it’s shock proof so you’re good! We will be tossing this in the Jeep and bringing it to the beach this weekend. Absolutely love that it’s waterproof and it just “weather resistant”. I wouldn’t worry a bit about tossing this baby on the boat and jumping off the boat with it when we pull up to the island after we drop anchor. No need for a dry bag for this. I am so super happy I picked this one up! Absolutely 5 stars! :)

reviews.title: Portable Speaker King
reviews.text: I've had this speaker for about a month now and am still impressed as I was day 1.  The Nyne Bass resides atop my desk, against a wall at home and is used daily during my studies.  I suppose if you are looking at a product with Bass in the name you are wondering how those deep notes sound.  First off, be real here; this portable speaker, just like Bose, Sony, TDK or Beats, is not going to give you the depth of a 12" or 15" subwoofer currently dominating the car stereos of those around you at the stop lights.  However; the Nyne Bass will work hard for you and stretch as low as possible for those deep notes.  Although, you are not going to feel those ultra-lows we love which reverberate our spine and induce a brain rattling high; there is some deep note reproduction you simply must be happy with.  This may be an odd statement to make but I don't know you and if you judge me I wont lose too much sleep, so here goes...If you place the Nyne Bass over your bathroom sink, the really-deep bass like that found in "Preacher" by One Republic or "We Are Young" by Fun will rattle your shower door - quite impressive.  I listen to a spectrum of music including The Dell-Vikings (50's), old school Ice Cube, One Republic, Mozart and Dead Mau5.  The mid-bass certainly hits and you WILL feel it when the Nyne Bass is turned up, and YES this speaker gets LOUD.  I read reviews for the Sony SRS-X5 which said if the Sony was played unplugged it would limit volume, the Nyne Bass has no such restriction; so unplug, take it outside, and enjoy the volume sipping on some Lemonade, Chardonnay, Pabst, Port, Schlitz Malt, Coke, Water, Maker's Mark or whatever.  The highs are very good, crisp with a touch of warmth and clear, on occasion transmitting details my $150 Sennheiser headphones are not able to.  The bluetooth pairs fast and easy and to my experience extends 30 feet through a wall.  I tried the Bose SoundLink and SoundLink Color as well as the Sony SRS-X5 at Target, and in my opinion lacked the depth found in the Nyne Bass reproduction.  Yes, the aforementioned are smaller in size but a few inches in physical dimension is well worth the musical dimension of those deeper notes.  There is also a USB port on the back to charge your device from the speaker's battery as well as a stereo port to couple those devices without bluetooth capability.  I charged it day 1 and listened for 8 hours a day for a week without recharging.  My negative criticisms are actually unreasonable: 1 the highs can become distorted at maximum volume and I want those ultra-deep notes.  Distortion happens to all speakers and the small surface area of the speaker can only move so much air, damn physics!  So until Nyne or Bose break the laws of physics, at which point we all need pour life savings into the company, I highly recommend the Nyne Bass.
reviews.author: AGBKEGVY2TKWHA2PJA3PAK4EZ5KA

reviews.text: I thought by the look and price of it that this would be an excellent sound system. Well, it is portable, very easy to use and pair, and looks fantastic....but the sound quality is terrible. The bass, even at a lowish volume, sounds distorted and fuzzy, creating a Lot of vibration and very little in terms of quality sound. For the higher end, it sounds tinny, as though coming through a can. Meanwhile, the overall detail in the sound is poor, creating a fuzzy output that makes nearly any genre of music sound flat. I have speakers a tenth this size that sound better. I would not recommend it.

reviews.title: Good for emergencies
reviews.text: I selected this radio to keep with the rest of our emergency supply kit. If you'd like to do the same, I'd recommend you charge the radio first before putting it away. You'll be working the hand crank for hours if you don't, and that's no fun if the power is out. Sound works pretty well, the light works great too. You'll want to hang onto the instruction because the buttons for the radio are not intuitive. I like how it has a usb output if you need to recharge your phone, but I too wish there was a storage area for the charging cable. I don't get a lot of direct sun where I am, so I don't think the solar panel would be that helpful for me, especially if there's a storm that knocks out the power. For the price though, I think this radio would be a good addition to your emergency kit.

reviews.title: Wow! This equals parent of the year award..!!
reviews.text:
Ordered this for my son and his 17th birthday!! Chose this specifically because of the subwoofers and boy, you would’ve thought we had purchased an entire home stereo system… He was over the moon happiest with this gift- and mind you, he also received a new PS4 gaming station!!

I was apprehensive how well the sub would sound in a small Bluetooth speaker and truly, it suits the needs we have perfectly. Those needs being… “I’m 17 with a few friends over (ha! Must remember we’re in the middle of COVID-19..!!) and we want to turn up music in my room, take the music to the pool… Or, just tell people we have our very own subwoofer! Sad but true, it’s all about bragging rights at this age… If this is any indication of the coolness factor, I’ve had two parents contact me asking what exactly was purchased to make us the coolest parents of the year!! That is The best endorsement I need.
As for functionality, I’m impressed the sound quality can turn an adequate size living room into a rave, mosh pit, country jamboree… Whatever your poison is- this speaker does not distort at the higher volume, which I was concerned about. The fact the music of choice happens to be mostly rap is another story… but, that is not the speakers fault it’s my sons poor taste in music!
As you can tell I am not a stereo guru or techie nope! Not in any capacity… I do know you can enter an SD card which was pretty impressive to the kids… I know they can lay tracks if that makes anyone feel better? Personally, I just made certain that had nothing to do with using drugs- and when I was assured it clearly wasn’t… I let them do their thing!
Great idea I want to toss out… We also chose a Bluetooth microphone that runs about the same price as the speaker- And.. as the kids listen to music they can karaoke of sorts… Using a lot of fun features the microphone has (Voice distortion, echo, voice enhancing.. truly the ability to make anyone sound better than they do!) I mention this because, together these two items have created such a fun source of entertainment for the small crowd… And during the quarantine I will take it all day long!
At night- my son plugs in the speaker so it’s fully charged and they have used it for a few hours at a time without any issue a battery‘s dying… Note, if next to an outlet you could also keep speaker plugged in and use it just the same. Technically, if plugged in this could run 24/7- but, not sure you want to torture yourself that way!?
I will admit, this does remind me a little bit of my old high school days in the 80’s.. when we would carry around a “boom box” on our shoulder- yep! You never knew when you needed to have a break dancing riff-off..!! The only thing different is, this Bluetooth speaker is half the size! Looks the same but a mini me version..
If looking for something to listen to that produces decent sound quality and definitely gets a throaty base this is it! Kids will appreciate it parents be warned-
I hope this info helps.. feel free to let me know!
reviews.author: AEIPJBAN7A55Q5DFFPZSR2UV3OKA

reviews.title: Impressive Sound from an Easy to Use Convenient Speaker
reviews.text:
I have tested and reviewed literally hundreds of Bluetooth speakers and while I am not a fan of music that is particularly bass heavy I found this speaker produces rich, vibrant sounds from all wave lengths. It produces more than ample volume for most any setting and has several connection methods.

While I have tested and reviewed Bluetooth speakers costing ten times (and more) what this does, the sound quality from this speaker is really quite good. In relation to the sound quality I would place them on par with mid to high end Bluetooth speakers.

In regards to charging time and battery life, both are outstanding. In my tests charging takes about approximately 2 hours to charge the built in 4000 mAh battery and the speaker lasts a solid 4.5 hours at max volume - 8 hour with volume midway (of course this will vary a bit due to exact settings).

There are multiple ways to connect to this speaker including Bluetooth 5.0, which can reach much further than previous versions, through an AUX cable, a 3.5mm jack, TF card, USB flash drive, or FM radio - it even has a built in extendable antenna.

While it has a remote and I tested it and it works fine, I rarely use it as I connect with my phone all the time.

I have taken this speaker with me to the pool for the last couple of months and have been thoroughly impressed with its performance. The battery, being 4000 mAh, is much longer lasting than most allowing for all day listening and the sound quality has been really good.

This is an all around great Bluetooth speaker that I certainly recommend.
reviews.author: AEP4CUO23WOOOEYSJBXWYLLHD5IQ

reviews.title: Surprisingly awesome!
reviews.text:
I've owned many affordable bluetooth speakers, but never one this size! I love this, the sound quality is FAR BETTER than anything I've used in this price range. The woofers deliver solid bass, and the directions really help fill a space with sound. I love that it even has a carrying handle and a place to plop your phone onto (great for Karaoke!). I'm a little confused by the Mic input, I thought perhaps this could work as an alternative to the line in... for example, I can line in for music, but also sometimes I'd like a mic-level input for using with a microphone. But the mic input seems to only get used for some sort of recording to TF card function, which I couldn't get to work anyway. Doesn't matter, I wasn't so interested in that feature, I was more interested in using it as an amp and thought that might be an interesting alternative use.
Using the speaker for both bluetooth audio or wired aux in worked very well. The FM radio is nice too, but I didn't try it much. All in all, the battery life seems pretty solid and this thing is incredibly powerful for a battery operated speaker. We usually don't max out the volume because that's too loud!
reviews.author: AGDSEYGSA5K664EUHWKV3ARDXO2Q

reviews.title: Looks awesome and performs quite well.
reviews.text:
I ordered the 'Portable Bluetooth Speakers Bluetooth Wireless 100ft Outdoor Speaker with Double Subwoofer, Rich Stereo, Support FM Radio MP3 Player Remote AUX EQ, 30W Bluetooth Boombox for Home Yoga Party Camping' for review and would like to share some of my thoughts.

First of all, I ordered this portable Bluetooth speaker more for the looks than anything else. I had recently remodeled my office with a sort of industrial/steampunk look and thought this would look awesome on one of my new industrial book shelves. The speaker really does have that retro, industrial look and to top it off, it actually sounds and preforms quite well. The Bluetooth 5.0 pairing is super simple on my Android device and I appreciate that they did use the Bluetooth 5.0 tech opposed to the older outdated versions. This allows for insync listening to streaming services without the annoying delay that the other older versions have. Now with all that said, the sound quality is good but not amazing but for the price of this speaker, it's a bargain considering the input options, build quality, Bluetooth 5.0 and included remote. Overall I am quite happy with this speaker and it's looks and performance for the price!

reviews.title: Wonderful Little Speaker!
reviews.text:
I am definitely impressed with this little Bluetooth speaker. For being so small it really puts out decent amount of volume and the bass is particularly impressive. The device is super simple to use if you are using it as a simple Bluetooth speaker for music. It's a bit more challenging if you are trying to use it as a speakerphone with your mobile phone. I'm not sure why anyone would want to use it for such a purpose, so I won't hold that against them.

Build quality is better than average for a Bluetooth speaker. It is solidly built and is mostly made from some sort of wood laminate. It's particularly dense though which gives it a nice weight and feel. It's very easy to carry around with the built-in handle. The remote is an IR type, so it doesn't have any range beyond line of sight. It's adequate and simple to use though.

Some of the more advanced features like the EQ are a bit complicated to access without the remote, which is the only reason I would not give the product five stars. There are just too many button press combinations to remember to access these functions. Other than that, it's a fantastic speaker though and the sound quality is much better than I would expect considering it's size and price.

reviews.title: First, this speak puts out AWESOME sound!
reviews.text: That is why we buy speakers, right?  However, while I am no expert at adding to my "smart home," I have had pretty good luck with most items, but this...I downloaded the HEOS app and I do have the speaker set up there...the problem is, I cannot seem to get it set up with Alexa.  I'm sure I'll figure it out, but for now, I'm mostly using it with my phone and my very old, but much-loved Creative Zen MP3 player (and a bluetooth transmitter attached to it) that has many songs on it that I've had for a long time.  I was able to get the Denon paired to my television, but as the instructions stated, because it is a wireless connection, the lip-syncing is not good.  Somewhere around 5-10 second delay from video to audio.  But back to the beginning - this speaker, above all others, has AWESOME sound!
reviews.author: AGBG3KK74IKWJNQVMQAGVBWJ7FAQ

reviews.title: Pretty Good
reviews.text:
First the sound, because if it's bad you probably don't care whether it's easy or hard to set up.

Denon is a brand name I respect, but there's a lot of weirdness that goes around in the evaluation of consumer speakers. My husband and son are sensitive and picky, so I asked them for their opinions on this. Over the wifi/BT there's a bit missing here and there. Sounds better loud than with the volume down. The bass isn't artificially boosted, and the reproduction is dry but true.

Playing noncompressed formats, it sounds much better and more like something you would pay quite a bit more for (although you could pay a lot more and get sound that is less good, as well).

On a scale of mail order plastic speaker 1 to studio monitor 10, it's about a 7.5, which is to say well worth the money, especially if lots of big boxes and wires aren't in your aesthetic.

As for set up, piece of cake with the app. I wish there were more functionality in the app, but it's still pretty good. Bonus points for finding the music on my home cloud server, which doesn't usually play well with others.

* Great clear sound for something of this size
* It has a built-in microphone so you can use it for calls
* Great for my backyard since it's weather proof.

* 3.5mm input jack if you don't have Bluetooth

Cons:

* No remote included
reviews.author: AFEMM7I5XXHN67QPRVXNSRZ2UDLQ

reviews.title: Tickle Me Turquoise!
reviews.text:
Okay -- Fugoo LLC calls it "Teal," but to me it is turquoise.

Number One: Great sound quality! Especially for such a small device. (The sound is 360° high fidelity audio.)

Number Two: Setup was easy, successful and "user friendly." I simply turned on the Fugoo Sport Wireless Speaker and turned on my ipod touch. They interfaced with each other quickly and automatically. (Surprise, surprise! I was actually dreading this procedure.)

Number Three: Has good volume -- I can hear it from at least 10 yards away. (It's a tiny "boom box.")

Number Four: Appealing design!

Number Five: Weighs a little more than one pound -- easy to "take with" by mounting it on your bike, kayak, or hanging it off of your backpack strap.

Number Six: Long-lasting battery, although it also comes with a plug for a wall outlet.

There's a new trend called, "The 100 Thing Challenge." If I can pare my possessions down to just 100 things, this Fugoo Sport Bluetooth Wireless Speaker is going to make my list.
reviews.author: AE3XHOX3OBSVFXACAPNFMKSUWR5Q

reviews.title: Loud, long lasting sound!
reviews.text:
Update: 2 years and still going strong!

Pros:
Portable for outside use.
Loud? Louder than the competition. I've tested its sound against the VAVA VOOM BlueTooth Speaker, and this is much louder.
Durable? The jackets provide that from what I've seen. The SPORT jacket has saved mine from three falls so far.
Musical? You'd better be playing some post-hegelian marxist prog-death industrial black metal on this.
Musical capability/quality? I've played a range of genres, ranging from black-bluegrass to old-school deathmetal to modern hardcore punk.
All sounded fabulous.
Have neighbors? Do you not like them? Blast the french national anthem at all hours while flying the flag of the Commune.

Cons:
Jackets' are rigid. Don't use the speaker without them, as they really do keep the speaker clean and protected.
Colors. The jackets are all the same color per tier of protection.

reviews.title: EXCELLENT sound! Fantastic price!!!
reviews.text:
This is a sizable and super affordable speaker from Altec Lansing that has truly wowed me. It is portable with a handle on top or you can make it stand. The outlet is protected in the back with a seal that you can open and close when using in or near water, but sitting it poolside is more than enough to me.

I tested it with just about every genre - hip hop, dance, classical, and even some Coltrane and the sound was crisp and clear with a punchy bass. I love speakers - I also have a Sony SRS-XB41 and this is right on that level, but even bigger and costs less, I think it can be compared to their party speaker. I love the lighting on this and the ways to play with it. The speaker also comes with decals and is neatly packaged with a start guide and power cord. I am truly going to enjoy using it this summer.

This is a great purchase for someone who doesn't want to spend TOO much on a bluetooth speaker but still wants quality sound, design and fun. Check it out!

reviews.title: Easy To Pair, Great Sound, and Very Versatile
reviews.text: I love this Bluetooth speaker. I own a few Sonos speakers and they are great for stationary speakers but it’s always good to have a few Bluetooth speakers especially ones this size that can go where ever you go. This is really great. The sound quality is amazing for this price. I bought this for my little girl’s playroom but I find myself carrying this around the house more often because it is so easy and accessible. There is absolutely no effort in pairing this to any device that offers Bluetooth pairing. Devices like this are really handy to have around. This has all the bells and whistles so it is also a stereo pairing speaker as well and can play music from a memory card that is 32 or lower. I really just use this as a Bluetooth device although I will probably record some nursery rhymes to a memory card for my l’il ones. This is a pretty nice product and comes in pink or blue perfect for a baby’s nursery which is what they marketed this on focusing on parents with young ones as the main target audience. It is really worth a lot more than what the price actually is so I would definitely recommend this to friends and family.

reviews.title: Not really loving the concept
reviews.text:
I listen to lectures on headphones when doing chores around the house and often can't hear people trying to get my attention. I've seen neck speakers before, and I thought it would be a very cool option for me as my family gets kind of annoyed that I can't hear them and they can't tell if I'm currently listening to something.
But I'll be honest, after trying these, I'm not really loving them. I had a pair of sunglasses speakers that aimed sound towards my ears, but this sort of blasts sound out and around me. Like, not even towards my ears. Basically, it seems just as loud to someone else in the room with me as it does to me, which then begs the question of why not just use an external speaker at that point? The call quality was just OK as well. The one thing I can say is that the build quality seems quite good. It feels really durable and made with premium feeling materials. But I just can't shake the feeling that for the asking price, I'm missing the point of these. Shouldn't the speakers be facing upwards towards my ears instead of blasting at each other in front of my Adam's Apple? Seems like a weird design that might be missing the mark.
reviews.author: AGDSEYGSA5K664EUHWKV3ARDXO2Q

reviews.title: Fantastic speakers
reviews.text:
I really like these neck speakers. It gives you a surround sound without having to use headphones that obstruct your ears. This to me is important especially at work or when I still need to be able to hear what is around me. I can easily use the controls without seeing them, since they are very simple to use. The volume can get very loud which is great so you don't have to give that up. These paired really easily with my phone and tablet so no issues there. They last a long time too which is great so I don't have to constantly charge them.

They are lightweight and I can barely feel that they are there. I wish they were a bit more flexible to be honest. I have some other sets as well that are a bit different but these are very good and I love using them.

reviews.title: No "Audio Controller".  Sound from the front only.  Made in Vietnam
reviews.text:

Having said that. . . . I like the sound. You have to download the Belkin Soundform App to set everything up. . . but there’s no “audio controller” to “tweak” the sound in any way. The app allows you to only stop/play, adjust sound, and so on.

The speaker is very easy to set-up through the app. Very easy to connect to Alexa.

If you have one of the many phones listed. . iPhone 8 and above. . .Samsung Galaxy. . .etc. etc. etc. . .you can place your phone on top and it will charge.

This speaker stands 6.75” tall in the back. It’s 6” across. It’s covered in a black “burlap” sort of fabric. It has a silicone bottom to keep it from slipping around.

Made in Vietnam.
reviews.author: AG375WAXLZ7PIOQKIQ6KQB4J3JVQ

reviews.title: Fills a very specific niche, though most would prefer another solution
reviews.text:
I rated this 5 stars for what this is, but note that what it is not likely means you likely want to move on.

What is it:
A combination wireless phone charger and desktop/nightstand speaker with Alexa (or Google Assistant)

If you're looking for all of the above, it may be for you!

What it is not:
A great stand-alone speaker
A great value vs a la carte options

If you want sound to fill a room, look elsewhere, as this is more of a nearby (desktop is a great use case) speaker
If your phone lacks wireless charging, there are cheaper smart speakers

As a speaker for my desk, it works well. I'm already immersed in the Echo/Alexa ecosystem, so another speaker is helpful and easy to setup. The sound is much better than any of my Echo/Ech Dot models, but not quite as good as some of my other Harman Karden or Bang & Olufsen speakers of a similar cost. Even adding an Echo Dot, audio cable, and a wireless charger, one of those speakers is a better value/experience than this one. Then again, that's three pieces of kit and some wires, and this does it all. That's the real story here--simplicity of design (if you need all of it's features) and execution. One box that does it all. With COVID, my wife and I work from home, and we have a couple workspaces we share based on the need. We have docks for our PCs at each location, but having this gem in the office is a nice plus. We can charge our phone, listen to music, and do whatever Alexa stuff we need (usually playing XM, Spotify, or whatever). It doesn't add anymore clutter as a 3-1 device vs any one of those, and it looks slick. I could see this as a great addition to a thoughtful guest room too, but that's about it.

So, it does what it does very well, but the individual components aren't great, especially if you consider cost. I cannot recommend this outside of a bedroom or office, but YMMV.

reviews.title: Packs a punch for the size.
reviews.text:
I ordered the iHome Alexa Built-in Bluetooth Speaker Portable Wireless Waterproof Rechargeable Lights Up to Music, with Speakerphone, Passive Subwoofer, Carry Strap, Durable Shockproof Floatable Design (IBT158) to replace an old sub par portable speaker that I’ve had for years. The iHome is quite the upgrade with the built in Alexa, better sound and being waterproof. I’ve had this product now for several weeks and would like to share my thoughts.

I must say that the sound is pretty good and packs a punch in such a small size. It’s much better than the much smaller and several years old portable wireless speaker that I have been using. In fact there really is no comparison at all. Let’s just say that it is the best sounding portable wireless speaker that I personally have ever owned. My daughter who “borrowed” the older speaker some time ago (and she never gave it back) keeps asking to "borrow" the new iHome as she says, “it’s louder and sounds WAY better”. I absolutely agree with her. Plus the lights on this thing are a nice touch, and there are options (via a button on top) to cycle through and change the color options or turn them off.

Setting up the iHome for Bluetooth and Alexa was easy and very straight forward, and there has been no issues whatsoever. We are mainly an Android household however my oldest daughter uses an iPhone and everyone has had the same great experience. So far my only complaint is that the Bluetooth connectivity distance isn’t the greatest but with that said, none of my Bluetooth devices really have much range in our home so I am not going to knock points off for that. The sound out of this little guy makes up for the Bluetooth short comings and the bonus of Alexa being packed into this as well as the waterproof features make this a great value for the selling price.

One thing worth mentioning is that to use Alexa, you can’t just say “Alexa” to activate her. You do have to physically press a button on the iHome speaker to access her, which isn’t a huge deal breaker, but because of this, you can’t use other Alexa devices to make an announcement and have it broadcast to the iHome speaker. Being fairly new to the Alexa and smart home world, I am not going to hold this shortcoming against the iHome device. It actually makes sense that with this being portable, you wouldn’t want to have Alexa always listening and drain the battery while on the go. Speaking of Battery life, it's decent but not spectacular by any means. This is pretty subjective however as usually when I use the iHome, I'm listening to it at a pretty high volume ;)

Another observation is that this uses micro USB to charge, and I really would like to see more devices using the newer and better USB C charging capabilities.

As far as the “waterproof” capabilities go, I must report that I have yet to float the device in a pool or let the kids play with it outside while running through the sprinklers. I know that it says it’s waterproof and it absolutely looks like it would hold up to getting wet as it is very well built, rugged and quite solid, but I don't want to risk ruining this new toy that the whole family has been enjoying so much.
reviews.author: AFLBMDZDIPCHMWWA7CXQXITPBJKA

reviews.title: It's pretty good
reviews.text:
Stutters some, and had to manually play next songs when I asked Alexa to shuffle music. Wish there was a way to connect it to Alexa without having to go through my phone. You really can't do much on your phone while you're connected obviously. The pings for pictures and calls and texts all come through the speaker. Even my Ring motion sensor alerts for doorbell come through while we were listening to music. So that's my gripe.
So on the other hand, the sound is pretty good to just listen to some tunes while we are hanging out in the pool and such. The lights are a cool feature. We are into lights. I like that it's durable for the light rain and being splashed poolside. All that is super great! So all in all I'd have to say I like it for what it is. And it will definitely work for backyard tunes!

reviews.title: Great for the shower but not a great all around speaker outside the shower...
reviews.text:
So for the shower- this things great! But outside the shower I'm just not impressed with this. I've reviewed several $20 ish price range bluetooth speakers and each of them has a little more bass and oomph than this one. It's pretty weak for what it is, even my Echo dot puts out more sound. That being said, this does get decently loud and is just fine for the shower! I used the attached suction and put it in my shower and it was great not having to reach outside the shower and touch my phone with wet hands to skip songs or commercials on youtoube.

Some things I've noticed on youtube though- I'll click the next button when it has a ad up and I can skip it. It'll then just send me to the next song as well and I'll have to hit the back button if I want to return to my original song. The ad will still be out of the way at this point so it WORKS for this, just kinda adds one more step. Just a minor annoyance but still so much better than having youtube take advantage of me being in the shower and cramming 2 to 3 ads at me at once since I often don't wanna reach outta the shower to hit skip every couople of minutes.

Battery lifes decent. For the price, it's not bad at all. Again, I'd really just use this for the shower. Maybe outside around a pool even... But if you're not using it for the waterproof feature, I'd skip it- the sound quality isn't impressive at all.
reviews.author: AE7YYSYFEWAYIUCAK4W33CHPP4JQ

reviews.title: Fantastic sound
reviews.text:
This was incredibly easy to pair. I paired it with my Kindle Fire HDX and it was faster than any Bluetooth pairing I've ever done before.

The sound quality is astounding. If I put it on the hardwood floor, and if I play something with a lot of base, and it I turn it up about half way, it will shake the floor. The range is equally impressive. It does not go tinny on the high notes. The sound remains clean and clear at all volumes (or did until my neighbor came to yell at me to turn it down). It produces music of a quality one would expect from a full sized unit.

The only thing to be aware of is that the speakers are along both long sides. That makes for fantastic sound, but the device must stand on the short edge while in use, which to my mind is not as stable. It uses a proprietary charger rather than charging via USB. Makes it just a tiny bit less convenient, but not a big thing. That's all I can say against it.

reviews.title: Nice little speaker!
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:bab23f3b60ae59f76540ed1bddb60fc6]] I've had a similar speaker to this for a few years now that is connected to my alexa device. Recently the sound started to get crackly and now it sounds like poor Alexa is suffering from some sort of vocal ailment.
It's bad. So I decided it was time to replace my speaker.
Things I like about this style of speaker- it's small! It fits in the palm of my hand. This means that it isn't intrusive in my decor. It also has very good sound and volume considering how small it is. I like it because I can take it outside with me and it stays connected to Alexa so I don't have to use my phone power for music, etc. Also, it is waterproof which is great as I live somewhere where it rains a lot. Could also go in the shower with you, but in my case I can hear it just fine without moving it.
I've not tested how long the battery lasts yet as I usually keep this plugged in. But the weather should be cooler this week so maybe we'll get some good gardening time in and I can test that out too.

reviews.title: Crystal clear, immersive sound!
reviews.text:
I wanted a home speaker with nice sound without a battery since it will be stationary most of the time. I also wanted an HD Bluetooth codec and line-in. Other popular "high end" brands, didn't bother to include the supported codec in their specs. This one supports AAC which is acceptable and supported by my Android phone. I wish it supported LDAC, but it's ok.

The sound is crystal clear, immersive and loud without any distortion/stress. Everything I was hoping for.

I mostly use it with Bluetooth, but you can cast using Spotify (and other apps that I didn't test/I don't use) and Wi-FI. When you do that, there is a lag between the Spotify App and the song playing on the speaker, so that's better to use when you don't plan fiddling and changing songs frequently through the app.
reviews.author: AEDRVFCAMFA64DPIOKHOWWN7CMRA

reviews.title: Fills the room with beautiful sound
reviews.text: I’m very pleased with this speaker. I set it up in my living room where I’m using it in place of a surround sound system and it fills that role well. I love that it has multiple ways of connecting to devices so it’s really easy to switch from the tv to somebody’s phone or tablet. This is the first speaker I’ve seen that offers both Bluetooth and WiFi connectivity. There’s also an audio jack for a physical connection. Controls are conveniently placed on top of the speaker.  Sound quality is exactly what I’ve come to expect from Sony. It’s excellent. The bass is deep and rich and the mid and high levels are clear and beautiful. You can customize the equalizer via the user friendly Music Center app. My favorite feature on this speaker is the 360 Reality Audio. Wow. It blew me away. I’ve listened to some nice speakers but never one that provided such a realistic experience. It is such a nice feature. I can crank it up and hear the music clearly throughout most of my home. It doesn’t distort at higher volumes either. I’m really enjoying this Sony speaker and highly recommend it.
