reviews.title: Great equipment, more capable than advertised! Outstanding value for the money.
reviews.text: This is an outstanding product! We've been using it for a few weeks and have been very pleasantly surprised. We thought this device would do a good job in a huddle/small conference arrangement. We've been able to use it in a larger (10 people) conference room with great success. We didn't expect that, because audio is a real challenge in larger spaces. The ConferenceCam Connect's microphones and speakers cover more territory than the specifications state. Video is sharp and the onboard compression processor really takes the strain off the laptop we use as a hub. We've noticed better performance on our Zoom videoconferences and the computer is able to produce a better image on our large screen because of the hardware support built into this Logitech device. Don't be scared off by the small size and low price, this is a very capable piece of equipment and a real value. One other item of note, it's very lightweight. We did buy a case for it, but the first two weeks we carried in the same messenger bag we keep the laptop in. All our conferences are less than two hours, so we've done everything on the built in battery so we only have to use one cable to hook up to the laptop. This one is a winner, highly recommended.
reviews.author: AHGE5INBATU55R5SXQKBZF64FSDA

reviews.title: Logitech Conference Camera is portable, powerful and easy to use video conferencing system.
reviews.text:
The Conference Cam Connect is a great device for video conferencing, it is compact, portable, and easy to set up. This video conferencing system connects easily up by either USB or Bluetooth and is ready to use right away. While being small and portable, it is full of features.
The camera delivers good quality video capability, the video is crisp and clear and bright, it does not have any framing or freezing issues that occur in other systems I have used. The ability to zoom and pan the camera using the remote is very handy feature on this unit. The microphone is good quality and picks up conservations from all angles very well, there are no apparent dead spots in its coverage. The speaker is also of good quality and delivers good sound quality, crisp and clear and not "tinny" sounding. The remote is a very handy addition to to the device and is the best way to control the unit, it allows panning, zooming, muting, and volume control.

The device is battery powered, compact, and portable, and connects wirelessly via Bluetooth. This allows you to move the device around to different areas without losing connectivity if so desired. It works great for small to medium size rooms and is compatible with all video conferencing software apps I have used to date.
reviews.author: AGE522R3Q7EE2UQ5VLYH4CT4VBBQ

reviews.title: Great Big Value for a such a little guy
reviews.text:
For $12 this speaker was an excellent value for the performance I got with it.

I charged it for about 2 hours fresh out of the box and it was full.
Pairing with my phone was quick and easy (held down the 'phone' key and the AI voice said "pairing"). Bluetooth ID is ISBW108
First I played the song Uptown Funk. (That wide range surely test any speaker.)
The high were very crisp and noticeable, mid range was very nice. The bass left a little to be desired but the volume was plenty loud for a personal speaker - especially one that's only one hand width tall.
I tried the speakerphone and it performed as expected with plenty of volume and clarity. The person on the other end said that my voice was just a bit muffled but otherwise clear.

For a $12 personal waterproof Bluetooth speaker / speakerphone that comes with a carabiner clip for belt or backpack - it's definitely a good value at this price.

reviews.title: Excellent radio, so nice I ordered a second
reviews.text:
It's the multifunctionality that really wins me over, and in such a small package. I've replaced my EDC bt speakers with these. Bluetooth volume and clarity are on par with Altec Lansing's mini-H2O speakers. Battery life is awesome, I can go 8-10 hours on mid-volume Bluetooth. It also runs & charges at the same time. Radios have good reception & favs is a good feature. I really appreciate the scan feature, especially with shortwave. Button lock is handy. Having a wheel in addition to seek buttons is a nice feature, as is analogue volume. The tf software has a lot to be desired, considering the lcd panel, they really should've offered a menu for sd contents.

I'd like to request, in the future, offer audio-out - either a headphone jack or ability to send radio over BT to a device... Basically, I want to be able to record, or at least use my galaxy buds to listen to shortwave, using my phone as the host. If you release an upgraded model with the aforementioned features and more radio bands (TV audio, CB, USB (upper side band), LSB, air, and marine) I'll be first in line for one.

reviews.title: Love this little powerhouse!
reviews.text: The reviewers who vouched for the good sound quality, thank you! Otherwise I wouldn't have ordered this mini powerhouse. First thing I did was tune in the local(-ish) jazz station that I can only get in my car and WOW was the sound clear! Had to use the antenna because the station is in Long Beach and I in NE Los Angeles but was very impressed. Admittedly I have not tried out every one of the myriad features of this - and I'm fine that it can't send out sound through its bluetooth, only receive. Having this may enable me to toss the terrestrial mini-radio and endless supply of D batteries that I had for emergencies, and it fits in the palm of my petite hand. Plus it's a badass looking little military-style thing that comes in two cool colors.

reviews.title: Small with lots of watts
reviews.text:
The unit is smaller than I was expecting and it's surprisingly dense/heavy and it's solid - there's no wiggle or things rattling inside.
It sounds like every bit of a 7w speaker. Maxed out with a volume meter sitting about 6ft away, I was steadily getting 90dB with peaks up to 110dB.
Sound quality is surprisingly good, even maxed out; of course with it being so tiny, there's basically no bass.
I'm not sure I'll use this for music very much, but it makes an excellent speaker for my bedroom TV - I can place it close enough that I can keep the volume down, being small lets it be placed just about anywhere, and the battery life is pretty good (I'm going on 8hrs of moderate volume).
I have no intention of using the TF slot, so it wasn't tested.

reviews.title: Perfect speaker for the garage gym - haven't tested the waterproof feature
reviews.text:
This speaker meets the needs for the garage gym at work and so far has really good sound and play time on it. I do turn off the flashing lights in an attempt to ensure I can get more music time out of it, and every now and then I will hit the color lights button to jazz things up a bit.
The speaker comes with a small piece of rope to put through a loop to use as a carrying handle (I didn't attach it). It has a 3.5 mm x 3.5 mm plug (both 'male' ends) to plug in an external source. The power cable is a USB to USB-C charging cable, and there is no 110 outlet plug associated with it, so ya know.
It was super easy set up, easy to connect (showed up as M8 in my bluetooth device finder) and the music was going within seconds of unboxing the speaker.
When switching playing devices (cell phones for this example) I had to go into my settings and disconnect from the speaker in order for another phone to "see" the speaker and then connect.
Happy listening.

reviews.title: Outstanding base response, volume can be maxed up to 11 w/o distortion of treble!
reviews.text:
Okay so I was very impressed with the speakers when I first got them, however... They have nothing like 14 hours of battery. I listen to them for a few hours and have to recharge them and now I'm having to recharge them twice a day. I'm also very disturbed to see that the price has doubled. My entire review was that these were good speakers for the price. But my JBL rarely has to be recharged and it's just as loud and bassy it's just not separate speakers.

But until I get around to that, know that these Paxcess Twins speakers are blowing my mind. It cost half of what I paid for the JBL Flip 2 years ago and it's louder and separate, sweet! And they're called the twins, what could be better? I have named mine Tiffany and Sabrina.

They have a big stereo speaker sound, and this TWS technology is awesome. Each speaker has a power switch, you turn them both on. Each speaker has volume buttons which act for both speakers whichever one you use.

If you had these in your cubicle or whatever office at work, you'd have to turn them down to a whisper.

Before I forget and this is important, when they're turned up loud they will creep on their rubberized feet, unless they're on something like marble or steel. So on some surfaces you may want some sort of rubber coaster so it doesn't fall off the edge.

reviews.title: Great rugged speaker
reviews.text:
This is a nice speaker, with good overall sound. I think it will survive in the elements quite well, as you can tell from construction - it's big, it's heavy, and it's definitely tough. This speaker is rated for some exposure to water, but you can't really put it in the water, so careful of dropping it in the pool. You can turn the volume up loud on this one as well, and it will happily respond.

I think my only issue is with the sound itself. I think that the majority of what I play on it has a kind of hollow sound to it. The bass is definitely loud, and if you play a bass-heavy tune, that sound will come out strong. I think for a more mellow tune, though, this one lacks in terms of sound quality. I suppose that this is an issue that will deal more with what you're listening to and where you're listening to it (obviously, acoustics will change if you're indoors or outdoors, depending on the size and shape of the area you're in), but I wasn't too impressed with it's range of sound options. Like most speakers of this kind, you can't really make any meaningful adjustments outside of where you place the device.

reviews.title: The Speaker Itself Is Good But Bluetooth...
reviews.text:
Bottom Line - The speaker itself is excellent but Bluetooth can be an irritating technology.

The speaker is lightweight and even has an attached carrying strap. Unlike a lot of lightweight products though, it doesn't feel flimsy or fragile at all. Instead it has a nice, solid, durable feel to it. While I am not an audiophile, the speaker has a deep, rich sound that I really like. It does well with audiobooks, music, and videos.

The problems seem to be with Bluetooth. One major issue is that the sound stutters while using Bluetooth. It's very noticeable when playing music and very irritating when listening to audiobooks. That's happened with every Bluetooth speaker I've ever tried. A google search indicates that it's a common problem related to the amount of information the computer is trying to pump out to the speaker. There's a checklist of possible issues to go through to try to fix the problem but the stuttering seems all too common with this technology.

Another issue involves changing modes on a Bluetooth speaker. To sidestep the stuttering I plugged this speaker into my computer with a standard jack. It worked fine and the sound was good. When I unplugged it to bring the speaker into another room with me, I had to redo my audio settings. When I plugged it back in with the jack, an audiobook played just fine but there was no sound while playing videos. Again, I had to go into the audio settings and fiddle with them to be able to watch a movie. I've had similar issues with other Bluetooth speakers.

If you are a techie you may be able to resolve such things quickly and easily. The less tech adept you are, the more hassle they will be though. Myself, I'm just going to leave the speaker connected to my pc via a good old fashioned jack because I like its sound. Bluetooth? Bah, I haven't got time to bother with it.

reviews.title: All things considered - meh with a battery pack.
reviews.text:
Don't get me wrong, the unit feels sturdy, has lots of features, and can get considerably loud. However, For $35 its a reasonably good value considering that the unit doubles as a portable 3000mA power bank to charge your portable devices on the go.
The sound quality is not stellar. The dynamic range is, well, squashed. The highs aren't really high and the lows aren't there. There are other units for this price out there with a much wider range and clearer sound (Oontz Angle) but if average sound quality doesn't really matter to you then you might give this a try and charge your phone while your at it.

Cons: It has a very musk-y smell. Like it was manufactured in a barn or farm yard. (I know what this smells like considering where I live.) (-2 stars) Very overpowering.

reviews.title: Love this speaker
reviews.text:
I love these types of speakers and this one works great. It is easy to use. It pairs great without any issues and is just so easy to use. The sound quality is great and the volume gets nice and loud. I don't usually use them for the phone so I am not concerned with that function nor do I test it but am sure it works just fine.

I like these because I can still hear what is going on around me which is especially important when I am at work. They still allow me to listen to music though, perfect solution. The price is also pretty good for it. All in all very happy with this one especially since it is very light and you barely feel that it is there.
reviews.author: AEZP6Z2C5AVQDZAJECQYZWQRNG3Q

title: Hercules WAE Outdoor BTP04 Adventure Pack IP64-Certified Bluetooth Speaker (Black)
description: Hercules WAE Outdoor BTP04 Adventure Pack Bluetooth Speaker The WAE BTP04 is perfect for enjoying all of your activities and outdoor adventures with your favorite music! Your music will follow you anywhere, without missing a beat: at the skate park, at the beach, on your bike trips, while you’re skiing! This single-unit Bluetooth 2.1 + EDR (A2DP) stereo speaker is compatible with Bluetooth devices including mobile phones and smartphones, tablets, and computers (Windows Vista/7/8, Mac OS X). The built-in lithium ion battery provides more than 10 hours of continuous play. The two active wideband drivers, plus one passive bass-amplifying driver provide excellent audio clarity. Rubber end-pieces provide enhanced shock resistance, and a double protective grille covers the drivers. A built-in microphone lets you enjoy Hands-Free calling. You can easily connect multiple WAE Outdoor BTP04 speakers to one another. The WAE Outdoor BTP04 includes a USB cable, snap hook, protective pouch and wrist-strap, and features a standard-size screw thread for easy mounting. This special “Adventure Pack” includes accessories that let you enjoy the ultimate in sports adventures and push the envelope of your performance with your favorite music! A bike mount, suction cup mount, backpack attachment clip and car cigarette-lighter charger are all included in this special pack. The WAE App for iOS and Android: Hercules also developed the "WAE Remote" - an extremely easy-to-use app that’s compatible with both iOS (4.0 and higher) and Android (2.2 and higher), providing listeners with another very convenient way to use the speaker. The WAE App helps manage Hercules speakers equipped with Bluetooth technology, and turns users’ wireless music experience into a simple moment of pleasure. This application is intuitive, user-friendly and rich with ingenious features. The WAE App allows users to easily control the speaker’s volume and effects, and to adjust the equalizer’s settings to perfect tone. IP6

reviews.text: Terrific. All one needs to have great music at a backyard, beach, etc party is a smart phone and this small speaker. Really cool!
